title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Release Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline for buildkit graphs, cache, multi-stage builds, mounts, and secret boundaries, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.
Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Buildkit Llb Graph
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Buildkit Llb Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain frontend-to-llb conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Buildkit Llb Graph, keep Docker Buildkit Llb Graph connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker External Cache Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-external-cache-reproducibility-grrcpd". Tailor its Dockerfile.buildkit change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker External Cache Reproducibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker External Cache Reproducibility into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker External Cache Reproducibility, keep Docker External Cache Reproducibility connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-external-cache-reproducibility-grrcpd
  - competency: docker-external-cache-reproducibility
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: external cache reproducibility
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: remove dependency readiness while preserving start order
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: grrcpd
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
